_how_to_update: Add a new object to the TOP of 'entries' every time the hub ships a change. Shape: { date:'YYYY-MM-DD', emoji, title, items:[{type:'new'|'improved'|'fixed', text}] }. 'upcoming' is a plain list of strings shown as a 'Coming soon' card — announce UI changes BEFORE shipping them, then move them into a real entry when they land. changelog.html renders this file — no other edits needed.
upcoming: The Optimizer starts learning from YOUR results — titles and tags that performed well for your channel get quietly boosted (your data, your device, just math).
Auto-filled view counts for posted videos via YouTube's free feeds, so the learning loop feeds itself.
Title ↔ thumbnail match check — the vision model measures whether your thumbnail shows what your title promises.
A '📈 your patterns' panel — best posting days, idea-to-posted speed, and your focus hours, computed from your own history.

entries.title: The Optimizer now shows your keyword opportunities

items.text: After you score a short, there's a new 'Keyword opportunity' card. It shows which of the phrases people actually search for you're already using — each with a simple 0–100 opportunity score — plus a few 'easy wins': words that get lots of searches but aren't too crowded, that you could work into your title or hashtags.

The score is honest and easy to read: how wanted a phrase is, dialed down by how crowded it is. The numbers come from your weekly keyword data, and the whole thing is worked out right in your browser — nothing about your video is sent anywhere.

entries.title: More kinds of creator to discover in the Niche Finder

items.text: Seven new content niches you can now get matched to: Gacha & Live-Service, True Crime & Mystery, Fitness & Movement, Beauty/Fashion & GRWM, Mukbang & Food, Cosplay & Costuming, and Pets & Animals — each with its own description and 'try these first' ideas, and tied to the tag bank so saving still powers the rest of the hub.

Two new vibe archetypes on the energy axis: Lovable Menace (you stir the pot on purpose and chat loves it) and The Showman (every stream is a produced show). Three new questions were added so all of these are actually reachable — the quiz is now fifteen questions.

entries.title: The Niche Finder is gentler to take now

items.text: The quiz now lets you tap an answer and then hit Next, instead of jumping ahead the instant you tap — so you can sit with a choice or change your mind. Each question eases in, the progress shows where you are in each part ('Content · 3 / 6'), and you can use the number keys 1–6 to pick and Enter to continue.

Your results screen got clearer: match meters on both halves of your recipe, and a 'close call' note when your top two are neck-and-neck (so it says 'you're a blend of X and Y' instead of forcing one).

Results now hand you a 'Start here' row — one tap into the Planner, the Optimizer, or the Growth Playbook, tailored to your niche — plus the existing save-to-Creator-Memory and copy-your-recipe. The honest 'day off' question now counts a little extra, since it's the best predictor.

entries.title: Buttons are easier to read everywhere now

items.text: Across the whole hub — planner, to-do, habits, weekly review, debut checklist, media kit, pitch builder, finance, growth, toolbox, thumbnail, FAQ, manager hub, Creator Memory, Ask and more — buttons and pills that used hard-to-read white text on the soft pastel gradients now use the deep brand ink instead. Same cozy look, but the text actually meets accessibility contrast now.
Dark buttons and overlays were deliberately left as-is (white text is correct there), so nothing went invisible — just the pale ones got fixed.

entries.title: Picking what kind of short you made is way easier

items.text: The big wall of 'what kind of short is this?' buttons is now a tidy search box: a few common ones to tap, or just start typing (e.g. 'cosplay', 'horror', 'ASMR') and it finds the match. Your picks show as little removable chips, so it never feels overwhelming even though there are far more options now.

Loads more categories to choose from, pulled from the hub's own tag bank so they map to real hashtags — formats like review, comedy skit, edit/montage, rant, life hack, true crime and bodycam, plus topics like horror, anime, cozy, cooking, fitness, beauty, tech, pets, mental health, ADHD, nostalgia, parenting, gacha, gunpla, LARP and more.

entries.title: The Optimizer got a calmer, clearer makeover

items.text: The Optimizer now opens with one simple choice — 'Score my short' or 'Generate titles & tags' — and shows only the tool you picked, so you're not staring at everything at once. It remembers your last pick, and there's a little 'change what I'm doing' button to switch.

Your results now lead with ONE thing: a 'Fix this first' card calls out the single highest-impact change before the full breakdown (which stays tucked away until you want it). Less overwhelm, clearer next step.

The score now counts up with a gentle pop when it lands (and stays still if you're in Focus / Low-stim mode), and the buttons and grade pills got the same readable dark-ink-on-pastel contrast fix as the rest of the hub.

Don't worry — nothing about how it works changed. The scoring, the title ideas, the way it reads your video, the copy-paste packs — all exactly the same. This was purely about making it calmer and easier to use.

entries.title: The hub feels a little more alive now

items.text: The hub now moves with a bit more life: pages gently glide in as they load, sections fade and rise into place as you scroll to them, dialogs spring open softly, and ticking off a checklist item gives a satisfying little pop. It's lively but calm.

All of that motion turns completely off the moment you switch on 🧘 Focus or Low-stim mode, or if your device is set to 'reduce motion' — so it's there when you want energy and gone when you want quiet.

More accent text is now easy to read: the active filter chips, the current-page button in the menu, and the quick-idea save button switched from white to the deep brand ink on their pastel backgrounds (white wasn't meeting the contrast standard). Same look, clearer text.

entries.title: Made the hub kinder to your brain and eyes

items.text: 🎯 New Focus mode, alongside the dark / dyslexia / tint / low-stim toggles. It quiets the busy background to one calm tone and gently dims every card except the one you're hovering or have selected — so your attention has one clear home instead of a wall of equal things. Saved per browser, off by default.

Better colour contrast on key buttons: text on the soft pink-and-blue gradient was hard to read (it didn't meet the accessibility standard), so it's now the deep brand ink, which is comfortably legible while keeping the pastel look.
Bigger, comfier tap targets on the shared buttons (a full 44px tall) so they're easier to hit — especially on a phone or on a rough-motor day.

Checklists are getting friendlier too: a little bar that shows how far you've gotten ('3 of 5 done!') with a happy moment when you finish, plus steps you can tick off with rough time guesses. It starts on the debut checklist, your to-dos, subathon prep and the planner.

entries.title: Everything feels a touch smoother

items.text: Buttons, chips, cards and inputs now ease in and out of their hover, press and focus states instead of snapping — small thing, but the whole hub feels softer to use. Nothing moved or changed shape; it's the same layout, just smoother.

Clearer keyboard focus outlines across the hub, so tabbing through with a keyboard always shows you exactly where you are. Fully respects 'reduce motion' (and the 🧘 low-stim mode) — if you prefer less animation, you get none.

🔄 Loading states are getting a proper polish. Save buttons can now show a tidy spinner while they work (the Planner's save button is the first to use it), and there's a new shimmer 'skeleton' placeholder and toast-message style that the rest of the tools will adopt page by page.

entries.title: A few fixes — including an important one for your privacy

items.text: 🔒 Your hidden media-kit prices are properly hidden now. If you'd marked a price as 'hidden', it stayed off the visible page — but it was still tucked inside the page's data where a nosy sponsor could have dug it out. Now those prices are never sent to the public version of your kit at all. Editing your own kit works exactly the same.

⏱️ Subathon donation time now respects fractional rules. If your rule was something like 'half a minute per $1', each donation was being rounded up to a whole minute, quietly adding more time than your rule said. It now adds the exact amount, so your time cap means what it says.

📊 Subathon timer overlay shows the goal bar even when your goal is typed with a $ sign. A donothon goal like '$500' used to make the whole progress bar vanish on stream; it now reads the number correctly (commas and units too).
🔍 Niche Finder's progress bar now matches the '# / 12' counter beside it instead of lagging one question behind.
💰 Finance: a fat-fingered business-use % no longer slips through as a blank value — unreadable input safely falls back to 100%.
🧹 Editor dashboard tidies up its live-updates connection when you sign out and back in, so a shared computer never ends up with a leftover feed from the previous person.

entries.title: Lighter on your machine — background pages stop spinning

items.text: The 📊 subathon timer overlay used to keep polling and re-drawing once a second forever — even when its tab was hidden in the background. It now quietly pauses while hidden and instantly re-syncs the moment it's shown again, so it stops burning CPU and battery on your streaming PC. The visible OBS overlay is unaffected; the countdown is always recomputed from the live total, so it's never wrong after a pause.

The Subathon Planner's live timer preview did the same once-a-second redraw forever in the background; it now pauses when the tab is hidden and redraws the instant you come back. Your real saved timer keeps counting server-side either way — this only affects the on-screen preview.

✨ Smarter search in Ask My Planner no longer lets a slow meaning-match from an earlier question overwrite the answer to a newer one — if you search again before the first finishes, only the latest result is shown.

The planner board now updates more efficiently when a card changes: instead of re-downloading your whole board (and re-loading your creator profile) on every single edit, it patches just the card that changed and redraws. Card order is identical to before, and anything unexpected safely falls back to a full refresh. You'll notice it most on big boards and when an editor is making lots of quick changes.

In the Thumbnail tester, the YouTube preview mockup no longer rebuilds (and reshuffles its example neighbour thumbnails) every time you tick a checklist box — it only refreshes when the title text actually changes. Less flicker, less wasted work.

entries.title: Machine learning ≠ generative AI — said properly now

items.text: The FAQ now explains the hub's tech precisely: it runs MACHINE LEARNING (speech-to-text, image understanding, meaning-based search, statistics over real performance data) — and contains ZERO generative AI. Nothing here invents content with a language model, nothing you make trains one, and the on-device models only ever recognize, never generate.

⚡ Fast mode in the Optimizer's video check — an optional smaller speech model (~40MB instead of ~770MB) so the first-ever video analysis downloads way less. Default stays the high-accuracy model; the toggle remembers your choice.

🌱 The planner now gives a gentle heads-up when a brand-new card looks like one you already have — it saves either way (capturing twice is normal!), it just points out the lookalike so you can merge or archive guilt-free.

entries.title: Everything from Coming Soon, shipped

items.text: ✨ Smarter search in Ask My Planner — turn it on and vague questions get matched by MEANING, not just exact words ('that spooky collab thing' finds the right card). One-time ~25 MB download, then it runs entirely in your browser; the instant answers stay instant, and keyword search still works if you leave it off.
🆚 Thumbnail A/B compare — score a thumbnail, hit 📌 Pin, score the other one, and get them side by side with the verdict and the biggest factor differences.

📤 'Sendable?' meter on the Media Kit — a completeness bar that says out loud when the kit is good enough to send (80%+), what the fastest wins are, and which bits are just nice-to-haves. Perfectionism doesn't get a veto anymore.
The Toolbox and What's New pages now sit on the exact same pastel canvas as every other page — no more odd-one-out backgrounds.

entries.title: The Toolbox — little brain tools

items.text: 🪚 Task breaker — type the scary task, get small physical steps, choose how tiny to chop them. Knows creator tasks (editing, thumbnails, scripts, taxes, going live) and has a kind generic recipe for everything else.
🎭 Tone shifter — paste a message and nudge it more formal, more casual, softer, shorter, or less sorry (the hedge-remover for over-apologizers).
⚖️ Tone read — a heuristic read of how your message comes across, with receipts: it shows exactly which words triggered the verdict so YOU stay the judge.
⏳ Time guesser — honest time estimates with the real-brain buffer built in, plus a 'block this much' suggestion so finishing early beats running late.

Everything in the Toolbox runs on hand-written rules in your browser — nothing you type is sent anywhere. Inspired by goblin.tools (whose lovely AI versions live at goblin.tools); the brain-dump goblin already lives on the To-Do list.
The Toolbox got front-door parking: a quick strip right under the home page search jumps straight to each tool, plus its own tile in Plan & Organize and a spot in the menu under More.

entries.title: The neurodivergent-friendly night build

items.text: 🧘 Low-stim mode joins 🌙 dark, 📖 dyslexia-friendly, and 🟡 soft tint — calm colors, zero motion, decorative art hidden. One toggle, every page.

Home now has 'Jump back in' (the last 3 places you were, one click to resume) and 'One thing now' — exactly one suggested next action from your own planner, todos, and review history. With an '↻ another' button if it picked wrong.
🧠 Brain dump on the To-Do list: empty your head one line at a time (each saves instantly), then sort everything ONE task at a time — Today / This week / Someday / Let it go.

Planner cards have a '▸ very next action' field — the literal next physical step, shown on the card and on your editor's dashboard. Quiet cards (14+ days untouched) get a gentle 🌫 chip, and dates now always show 'in 3d' next to 'Jun 15'.
Weekly Review has a 🪷 one-question-at-a-time mode — same nine questions, one screen each, with dots showing where you are.

🎙️ Dictation mic on the hub's big text boxes (browser speech recognition — free, nothing leaves your machine) and the Optimizer now autosaves your draft inputs, so tab-away never loses a thought. The top title suggestion also gets a ⭐ Eggie's pick badge for low-decision days.
Your Habits energy check-in (🌙/🌤/🌞) now travels: on low-energy days the To-Do list gently suggests picking just one thing.

Kanban column headers explain exactly what each status means and who can see what. The Manager Hub states, in plain words, exactly what managers can and cannot access. Creator Memory shows exactly which tools read each part of your profile.
This page now has a 🔭 Coming soon card — interface changes get announced before they ship, never as a surprise.

Pitch Builder: a ⏰ follow-up radar flags sent pitches that have sat quiet for 7+ days, and a 🧰 scripts pack has send-ready wording for the hard moments — declining politely, raising your rate, asking for clarity, nudging a ghost.

entries.title: Editor downloads fixed

items.text: Editors couldn't download files that were attached while a planner card was first being created (only files added to already-saved cards worked). The sharing rules now cover both — every file listed on a card you're assigned to downloads properly.
If a file genuinely isn't shared with your editor account, the editor dashboard now says so instead of suggesting a refresh that never helps.

entries.title: Optimizer V5 — one card, no steps

items.text: The Optimizer's 3-step wizard is gone. Everything is one card now: say what your short is about, optionally add a working title or your video, hit ✨ Optimize — score, critique, stronger titles, hashtag mix, and your post pack all come back in one flow.
Results lost their tabs too — suggestions and the copy-paste post pack stack right under your score, with the full score breakdown and resource library tucked into expandable sections.
Your inputs stay on screen next to the results, so you can tweak the title and re-optimize without starting over.
Sending a title over from the Planner now lands directly in the card — no step-hopping.

entries.title: The hub becomes an app

items.text: Install the hub like an app! On phone or desktop, your browser now offers 'Install / Add to Home Screen' — the hub opens in its own window and core pages keep working offline.
⚡ Quick idea capture — the menu on every page now has a 'Quick idea → Planner' button. Type a title, hit save, and it lands in your planner's Idea column without leaving the page you're on.
📜 This page! What's New keeps a running history of every hub update from now on.
The floating focus timer can now collapse into a tiny bubble — click – to shrink it, click the bubble to bring it back. It remembers your choice.
A new shared core (hub-core.js) powers all of this — future pages get the standard helpers and sign-in handling for free.

entries.title: Deep audit day — fixes everywhere

items.text: Switching browser tabs and coming back no longer reloads your data mid-edit on ANY page (it could silently wipe unsaved changes in Creator Memory, Weekly Review, Pitch Builder, Subathon setup, and more).
The 'Edited' status pill in the planner's view modal now has its proper green styling.
Editor emails are now normalized everywhere — editors with mixed-case emails could previously see an empty dashboard.
Ask-my-planner's server function queried two columns that didn't exist; manager view there works again.
Managers can now see a creator's Tweets planner and to-do categories (was silently empty before).
Internal docs corrected so future updates are built against the real database shape.

entries.title: V2 launch tools

items.text: 🎭 Debut Checklist — every step from 'I want to be a VTuber' to debut day, with progress saved to your account.
🔍 Niche Finder — a quiz that figures out your content × vibe recipe and saves it straight into Creator Memory.
⏱️ Subathon Planner — rules, goals, incentives, schedule and prep checklist, plus a live timer widget you can drop into OBS.

entries.title: Media kit edit protection

items.text: The Media Kit no longer resets your unsaved edits when you switch tabs and come back.

entries.title: Title engine V4 + home redesign

items.text: The Optimizer's title generator got a much bigger template bank, smarter batching, free synonym enrichment, and a 'show more' button.
The home page got its big redesign — tool tiles with search, grouped by what you're trying to do.

entries.title: Sponsor Kit V4

items.text: Media Kit — a shareable, public stats page for sponsors, with your branding.
Pitch Builder — writes your sponsor outreach (email, DM, follow-ups) from your media kit + Creator Memory.

entries.title: Spring 2026 — the foundation

items.text: Where it all started: the Shorts Optimizer and Content Planner core, then editors & delegation, manager co-ownership, To-Dos with focus timer, Sustainable Habits, Weekly Review, Ask My Planner, Finance & Tax, Growth Playbook, Creator Memory, accessibility modes, and the demo sandbox.
